description:
First half of High F2 (performance). Coverage output is consumed in exactly two places today (enumerator + JSON report) and never turned into a line->test mapping, so per-mutant test selection is impossible. Build the index.

File: lib/mutagen_ex/coverage_runner.ex (gather_covered_lines/analyse), threaded out alongside covered_lines.

## Approach (per-test-MODULE granularity — the cheap, high-value cut)
Run cover once per cited test module (or capture per-module deltas) so the result is %{scope_file => %{line => MapSet.t(test_module)}}. Per-test precision (one cover run per test) is a later refinement; per-module already collapses N -> "modules touching this line". Document the up-front cost (one cover pass per module) and that it amortizes across S sites.

## Done
- CoverageRunner produces the line->{test module} index for the scope under test.
- Empty/missing entries are representable (drives the safe fallback in F2b).
- Unit test pins the index shape against a small fixture with two test modules touching disjoint lines.
title: F2a: CoverageRunner emits a line -> {test module} index
